stem: A list called temperatures contains the daily high temperatures for a fortnight.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: best initialised to the first element (or to a sentinel that survives every value), loop covers every element, comparison + update, output. Allow `max(temperatures)` but credit the understanding marks only when the candidate also writes equivalent logic.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write a program that finds and prints the highest value in the list `temperatures`. Do not use the built-in max() function.

mark_points.text: running maximum initialised to the first element (or equivalent)
comparison updates the running maximum when a larger value is found
